note: B.3 selector-less (bare native transfer) routing. The HYPE deposit is a bare native transfer: EMPTY calldata + value > 0 sent to the HyperEVM system address 0x2222...2 (chain 999 mainnet / 998 testnet), hitting its payable receive(). It has NO 4-byte function selector, so it is keyed under the reserved sentinel selector 0x00000000 — the all-zero 4-byte word, which still satisfies the '0x' + 8-hex SELECTOR_RE that build-index + the SW bundle parser enforce. The WASM route (declarative_route_request_v3_json) substitutes this sentinel for the lookup key ONLY when calldata is empty, so it can never collide with a real dispatch (a genuine selector requires >= 4 calldata bytes). There is no native-value-transfer TokenAction variant (token actions cover approve/permit/transfer of a NAMED token only), so the honest body is ActionBody::Unknown: $calldata resolves to '0x' and $tx.value carries the HYPE amount, surfacing the full native value movement to the policy layer.
